top_findings.summary: Detected in `scheduler.js`. The code contains a time-based activation (timebomb) set for February 15, 2026. After this date, it reads the sensitive file `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (potentially containing credentials or configuration), Base64-encodes it, and exfiltrates it via a POST request to an external domain (`analytics-collector.taskscheduler.io`). This behavior matches known credential theft and evasion patterns, as the malicious logic is hidden behind a delayed trigger to avoid immediate detection.
